threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'local' means the pub on your corner, not the corner shop where you buy your dinner.
Caught a whiff of fried plantains last night; turns out the guy in the back had a whole stash—just him and a boiling hot Tupperware.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a grandma arguing with a pigeon over a piece of bread on the U7 platform, I realized Neukölln had a magic all its own.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a bakery that serves fresh pretzels alongside vegan kebabs at 2 AM. It's a weirdly perfect pairing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their willingness to wait for the 14-Mission instead of hopping into a rideshare at the first sign of rain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw someone serenading the 14 bus line regulars with a guitar, while a pigeon tried to steal their sandwich. Classic Mission vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the good tapas spots are in the guidebooks, but the best ones are tucked away in the side streets, often with no signs at all.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stand confused at the tram stops, watching the schedules like it's a foreign language. Meanwhile, the locals just flow with it.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to carry an umbrella in the sun. Here, it’s always about dodging the rain while the sun somehow beams down.
Last Saturday, saw a lad trying to impress his mates by singing 'Wonderwall' on the 199. The bus driver just shook his head and turned the volume up.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was truly living here when I saw a kid using a broken skateboard as a makeshift surfboard in the canal. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bustling market where you can buy fresh falafel and hand-knitted socks in the same aisle. It's a sensory overload!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they avoid eye contact with Muni buses trying to squeeze through 16th and Mission at rush hour. Total pro move.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 Mission, tying his bow tie and sipping iced coffee like it was totally normal. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are always buzzing, but try visiting on a rainy Tuesday—it's a ghost town and the locals cherish that quiet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them at the market, trying to haggle over the price of pa amb tomàquet like it’s a bargain, not a tradition.
